task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI application is open
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the data source is set to SYNTHETIC (Algorithmic)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data source selection or system control panel indicates 'Synthetic' or 'Algorithmic' is the active data source.

task_completion.description: Verify the Cyton board is selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface displays indicators of the Cyton board, such as the 'Cyton' label or an 8-channel/16-channel layout.

task_completion.description: Verify the Hardware Settings panel is open
task_completion.visual_evidence: A panel, widget, or dialog specifically titled 'Hardware Settings' is visible in the OpenBCI GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify the board mode is configured to Analog
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'Hardware Settings' panel, the board mode or auxiliary input setting is explicitly set to 'Analog'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the genuine OpenBCI GUI interface rather than a text editor, image editor, or mock application.

integrity.description: Verify the configuration was not bypassed using terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The settings are visible within the graphical interface of the OpenBCI GUI, not just typed as commands or JSON edits in a terminal window.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot appears to be a genuine capture of the agent's desktop
integrity.visual_evidence: The image includes typical desktop environment elements (like window borders or taskbars) or matches the expected rendering of the OpenBCI GUI on Ubuntu, without signs of being a downloaded stock image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is verified as '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/analog_mode_config.png'. The VLM must verify that the 'Hardware Settings' panel is open and the target mode is explicitly set to 'Analog'. The data source must be configured to 'SYNTHETIC (Algorithmic)'.
